title: The End of the Tunnel — Case File 5: The Door That Was Never Sealed
description: Case File 5 — Senior Detective: The final chapter reveals the mastermind behind the working tunnel route, blackmail drops, and forged history.

answerKey.method: Carbon monoxide released into the private service room while the door was locked from the bar side
answerKey.motive: To silence the blackmail broker and keep control of the tunnel operation
answerKey.keyEvidence: the bar-side lock log, the missing CO detector battery, and Vivian’s master key imprint
answerKey.explanation: Vivian Cross is responsible. the bar-side lock log, the missing CO detector battery, and Vivian’s master key imprint proves the method and timeline. This chapter reveals: The tunnel was never truly sealed; Vivian used the legend to control a blackmail and artifact network.

publicClues.title: Case File 5: The Door That Was Never Sealed
publicClues.text: Case File 5: The Door That Was Never Sealed begins inside The Tunnel. This is one chapter in a larger story, not a separate remake of the same mystery.

publicClues.title: The New Death
publicClues.text: The victim is Darius Vale. The old Peacock Alley legend still matters, but this chapter adds a deeper layer to the ongoing investigation.

publicClues.title: Checkpoint 4 Available

publicClues.text: Final questions are open. Close this case file and reveal how it advances The End of the Tunnel.

phone.title: Phone Evidence 1
phone.text: Darius called Vivian and said: “Your people are breaking. I need a bigger cut.”

phone.title: Phone Evidence 2
phone.text: Vivian’s phone records show one deleted call during the service-room lockout.

messages.title: Messages Evidence 1
messages.text: Darius texted: “I kept copies of Page 47, the envelope, and the work order.”

messages.title: Messages Evidence 2
messages.text: Vivian replied: “Come through the staff door. No cameras.”

maps.title: Maps Evidence 1
maps.text: Darius’s phone stops inside the private service room, not inside the old tunnel.

maps.title: Maps Evidence 2
maps.text: Vivian’s device stays at the bar, close enough to the service lock panel.

bank.title: Bank Evidence 1
bank.text: A blackmail payment stream routes through sponsor-pop-up vendor accounts.

bank.title: Bank Evidence 2
bank.text: The final account owner is tied to Vivian’s private event company.

photos.title: Photos Evidence 1
photos.text: A photo from earlier shows the CO detector battery compartment closed.

photos.title: Photos Evidence 2
photos.text: A later photo shows the detector missing its battery after Vivian entered the service hall.

social.title: Social Evidence 1
social.text: The official Tunnel account posts a nostalgia story exactly when Darius disappears.

social.title: Social Evidence 2
social.text: The post creates a crowd around the main room while the staff hall stays empty.

contacts.title: Contacts Evidence 1
contacts.text: Darius saved Vivian as “Owner — controls all doors.”

contacts.title: Contacts Evidence 2
contacts.text: A note says: “She built the legend into the business model.”

notes.title: Notes Evidence 1
notes.text: Detective note: by now, every previous case points upward to someone with authority over the venue.

notes.title: Notes Evidence 2
notes.text: Detective note: the final killer did not need to run through the tunnel. She only needed to lock the correct door.

files.title: Files Evidence 1
files.text: The lock log shows the service room was sealed from the bar-side control panel.

files.title: Files Evidence 2
files.text: The export was renamed “sponsor inventory” before being hidden in the host files.

browser.title: Browser Evidence 1
browser.text: A search asks whether old service rooms need carbon monoxide sensors after renovation.

browser.title: Browser Evidence 2
browser.text: Another search asks how long lock logs remain in a venue access system.

rounds.title: Final Accusation
rounds.objective: Close Case File 5: The Door That Was Never Sealed and reveal how it continues The End of the Tunnel arc.

openingNarration: Detectives, welcome to Case File 5: The Door That Was Never Sealed. Four cases have built the same map: forged history, ledger codes, King Hotel access, and Heidelberg movement. Now blackmail broker Darius Vale is dead inside a private service room. The final chapter asks who controlled every door from the beginning.
revealScript: Vivian Cross killed Darius Vale to keep control of the tunnel operation. She let everyone chase singers, archivists, event directors, contractors, and collectors while she controlled the room, the locks, the sponsor story, and the legend. The tunnel was never sealed. It was managed.

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 1: Why does Darius’s death connect to all previous cases?

options.label: A detail that sounds dramatic but does not prove the case
He held copies of the ledger page, envelope, access files, and work order
A rumor from the old hotel story with no physical support
A coincidence that does not affect the timeline

options.label: A detail that sounds dramatic but does not prove the case

options.label: A detail that sounds dramatic but does not prove the case

questions.prompt: Checkpoint 5: What is the final truth of The End of the Tunnel?

options.label: A detail that sounds dramatic but does not prove the case
The tunnel was never sealed; Vivian used the legend to run the operation
A rumor from the old hotel story with no physical support
A coincidence that does not affect the timeline
A person tied only to the old rumor
Vivian Cross
A guest with no access evidence
A victim from an earlier case file

questions.prompt: Final Accusation: What method was used?

options.label: A random accident during the crowd movement
Carbon monoxide released into the private service room while the door was locked from the bar side
A fight outside the hotel lobby
A ghost-story scare that went wrong

questions.prompt: Final Accusation: What was the motive?

options.label: To cancel the speakeasy event
To silence the blackmail broker and keep control of the tunnel operation
To steal an ordinary bar tab
To prove Peacock Alley never existed

questions.prompt: Final Accusation: Which evidence combination proves it?

options.label: A cocktail umbrella, a playlist, and a menu typo
the bar-side lock log, the missing CO detector battery, and Vivian’s master key imprint
A parking ticket, a rumor, and a receipt

questions.prompt: Final Accusation: What larger truth does this chapter reveal?

options.label: The case is only about a confirmed historic romance
The tunnel was never truly sealed; Vivian used the legend to control a blackmail and artifact network
No one has used the passage in modern times
The murder is unrelated to The Tunnel
